headline: Dassault Systèmes Deepens AI Role In Drug Development And Manufacturing
summary: Medidata, a Dassault Systèmes (ENXTPA:DSY) brand, received top innovation ratings for its AI driven clinical trial solutions and a Luminary designation for its role in FDA novel drug approvals. Medidata’s AI platforms are used across a large share of FDA novel drug approvals, supporting trial efficiencies and digital workflows in clinical research. Dassault Systèmes expanded its healthcare footprint through a new collaboration between Medidata and CRIO focused on clinical data...

headline: Nokia’s AI MWC26 Push Tests Long Term Growth And Margin Story
summary: Nokia Oyj (HLSE:NOKIA) used Mobile World Congress 2026 to announce a series of AI-led collaborations and product launches. The company unveiled new AI-RAN, open network automation, and agentic AI network services with partners including Telia Finland, Google Cloud, Deutsche Telekom, TIM Brasil, and Nvidia. Nokia also introduced next generation, AI ready radio products with a focus on energy efficiency and sustainable network build outs. Nokia enters this AI push with its shares at €7.032...

headline: What Intel (INTC)'s Board Transition and AI Partnerships Shift Means For Shareholders
summary: Recently, Intel announced that longtime board chair Frank Yeary will retire after the May 13, 2026 shareholder meeting, with semiconductor veteran Dr. Craig H. Barratt becoming independent chair, while Infosys and Corvex revealed new AI collaborations leveraging Intel platforms and Intel Trust Domain Extensions to secure and scale enterprise and GPU-accelerated workloads. These moves highlight Intel’s push to embed its CPUs, Gaudi accelerators, foundry security features, and 18A roadmap more...

content.title: What Intel (INTC)'s Board Transition and AI Partnerships Shift Means For Shareholders

headline: Nvidia halts China-bound H200 output, shifts TSMC capacity to Vera Rubin, FT reports
summary: Nvidia has stopped production of its second-most advanced artificial intelligence ‌chips, known as H200 chips, intended for ‌the Chinese market, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.  The ​U.S. chipmaker has reallocated manufacturing capacity at chip contract maker TSMC away from making H200 chips to its next-generation Vera Rubin hardware, the ‌report said, ⁠citing two people with knowledge of the matter.  Nvidia and TSMC did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

content.title: Nvidia halts China-bound H200 output, shifts TSMC capacity to Vera Rubin, FT reports
content.summary: Nvidia has stopped production of its second-most advanced artificial intelligence ‌chips, known as H200 chips, intended for ‌the Chinese market, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.  The ​U.S. chipmaker has reallocated manufacturing capacity at chip contract maker TSMC away from making H200 chips to its next-generation Vera Rubin hardware, the ‌report said, ⁠citing two people with knowledge of the matter.  Nvidia and TSMC did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

headline: CrowdStrike’s Record ARR And AI Security Push Reshape Growth Story
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings (NasdaqGS:CRWD) reported record fiscal 2026 results, surpassing US$5b in annual recurring revenue. The company posted its first GAAP profit in Q4 alongside record net new ARR, reflecting operational execution at scale. CrowdStrike launched new products, including FalconID, aimed at zero friction, AI resistant authentication as AI driven attacks increase. Partnerships with VAST Data and NVIDIA, plus new integrations with Qualtrics, extend CrowdStrike's role in securing...
